mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals resilient household demand and stronger near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month RSAFS trend has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS-trend months do not outperform negative-trend months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 1.00%, discount-rate pressure rises from a low-rate base and equity duration is repriced lower, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle from low nominal rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals expanding labor income without recession stress, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition fails to deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than all other labor states in at least 55% of walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in industrial production signals weaker real output and margin pressure, causing cyclical earnings expectations to fall and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average by more than 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-break triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and lower future construction-linked spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month HOUST declines greater than 10% are not associated with lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while the policy rate remains below 1% signals a negative real-rate gap and future policy catch-up risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS-below-1% trigger has nonnegative mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or no negative Rank IC in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment crosses upward by at least 0.3 percentage points from a sub-5% level, the labor market is entering late-cycle deterioration and equities deliver negative returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3pp while UNRATE < 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if upward UNRATE triggers from below 5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or have no downside skew versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP, industrial production, and retail sales signals broad macro acceleration that improves earnings breadth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0 and RSAFS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the broad-acceleration trigger has average 5-day S&P 500 returns less than or equal to unconditional average returns across walk-forward windows.
